title: Protecting American Energy Production Act
plain_summary: This bill stops the President from banning hydraulic fracturing across the entire country unless Congress passes a law that allows the ban. Hydraulic fracturing is a way to get oil and gas from underground by pumping water, sand, and chemicals into rocks to crack them open. The bill says that state governments should be the main ones in charge of making rules about hydraulic fracturing on state and private land. This means states would have more control over this type of energy production in their areas instead of the federal government.
last_action_text: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources.
subjects: Presidents and presidential powers, Vice Presidents

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Protecting American Energy Production Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Protecting American Energy Production
sections.text: Congress believes that state governments should have the main authority to make rules about hydraulic fracturing. Hydraulic fracturing is a method of drilling that pumps water and chemicals underground to break up rock and release oil and natural gas. This applies to oil and gas drilling on land owned by states and private companies. The President cannot stop or ban hydraulic fracturing across the country unless Congress passes a separate law that gives the President that power. This rule applies even if other laws might suggest the President has this authority.
